channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Republicans deny Democrats' requests for El Salvador oversight trips
item.description: House Republican committee chairs are denying Democrats’ requests to travel to El Salvador and visit its CECOT prison facility as scrutiny builds on the Trump administration’s handling of Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s mistaken deportation.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: FSU shooting victims include a school employee whose dad was a Cuban exile turned CIA operative
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Two people were killed and six others were injured when a gunman opened fire at Florida State University, sending students fleeing from the student union and putting the Tallahassee, Florida, campus under lockdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abrego Garcia told visiting US senator he was no longer being held at notorious Salvadoran prison
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen was flying home from El Salvador on Friday after meeting with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was wrongly deported there by the Trump administration. It’s unclear what will happen next in the case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Shooting rampage at Florida State that left 2 dead lasted less than 5 minutes, police say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Students stood in prayer circles and piled balloons, candles and teddy bears along a sidewalk Friday near Florida State University’s student union, where two people were shot and killed and six others were wounded during a rampage lasting less five minutes.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montana reports first measles cases since 1990
item.description: Two children have died amid the outbreak this year and a third death is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US has 800 measles cases and outbreaks in several states. Here's what you should know
item.description: The U.S. has 800 cases of measles nationwide as of Friday, and two more states identified outbreaks this week. Texas is driving the high numbers, with an outbreak centered in West Texas that started nearly three months ago and is up to 597 cases.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Deliberations to resume Monday at bribery trial of Nadine Menendez, wife of ex-US Sen. Bob Menendez
item.description: A jury will resume deliberations on Monday in the bribery case brought against Nadine Menendez after a prosecutor told a jury that she was a “critical participant” in a five-year fraud with her husband, former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Video shows doctor with measles treating kids. RFK Jr later praised him as an ‘extraordinary’ healer
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — A Texas doctor who has been treating children in a measles outbreak was shown on video with a measles rash on his face in a clinic a week before Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. met him and praised him as an “extraordinary” healer. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FSU shooting: Sheriff’s office speaks on suspect’s past, deputy stepmother
item.description: The Leon County Sheriff's Office is speaking out Friday after the suspect of the Florida State University shooting was identified as the stepson of one of its deputies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A US citizen was held for pickup by ICE even after proving he was born in the country
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A U.S. citizen was arrested in Florida for allegedly being in the country illegally and held for pickup by immigration authorities even after his mother showed a judge her son’s birth certificate and the judge dismissed charges.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two Marines
item.description: The two service members killed this week in a "vehicle accident" were 22-year-old Lance Cpl. Albert A. Aguilera and 28-year-old Lance Cpl. Marcelino M.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harvey Weinstein can stay in hospital during #MeToo retrial, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein has been moved to a New York City hospital after a judge approved the ailing ex-studio boss’s request to stay there rather than in jail when he’s not in court for his #MeToo retrial.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oklahoma City to mark 30 years since the bombing that killed 168 people and shook America
item.description: A bomb with a force powerful enough to instantly destroy much of a nine-story building shattered a quiet Oklahoma City morning and sent a shock wave through America.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: El Salvador president responds after Dem visits alleged MS-13 member and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctor in Germany accused of 15 murders, suspected in 95 more
item.description: A 40-year-old doctor practicing palliative medicine in Germany has been charged with killing 15 of his patients and is suspected in another 95 cases, NBC News reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Florida State gunman used deputy mom's former service weapon to kill 2 and wound 6, authorities say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The 20-year-old son of a sheriff’s deputy opened fire Thursday at Florida State University with his mother’s former service weapon, killing two men and wounding at least six others, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina school bus crash victim identified, 5 other students in critical condition
item.description: Multiple Lexington County, South Carolina, students were taken to the hospital after a school bus flipped on a the highway Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court keeps hold on Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship but sets May arguments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday kept on hold President Donald Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship but agreed to hear arguments on the issue in May. Trump’s executive order to end birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, KRON4

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: Check your Venmo, email and mailbox: A payment from a years-old class action lawsuit against Facebook may be on its way this week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about the shooting at Florida State University
item.description: Students and staff have returned to buildings on the Florida State University campus to retrieve their belongings a day after a gunman opened fire, killing two people and wounding at least six others.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Student who shot 4 at Dallas high school was targeting specific student, school police chief says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student who shot four students when he opened fire at a Dallas high school this week was in and out of the building in less than two minutes and is believed to have been targeting a specific student, the school district police chief said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, KRON4

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Menendez brothers hearing moves forward, albeit with confusion
item.description: The resentencing hearing for Eric and Lyle Menendez moved forward Thursday morning despite a motion for delay filed by Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: 3 dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: DOGE targets a community service program in its latest cost-cutting effort
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — A 30-year-old community service program that sends young adults to work on projects across the U.S. was the latest target of the Trump administration 's campaign to slash government spending.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration issues order to stop construction on New York offshore wind project
item.description: The Trump administration issued an order Wednesday to stop construction on a major offshore wind project to power more than 500,000 New York homes, the latest in a series of moves targeting the industry.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How war, money and the quest for discovery entwined the US government and universities
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The showdown between the Trump administration and Harvard University is spotlighting bare-knuckled politics and big dollar figures. But in the battle of the moment, it’s easy to lose sight of a decades-long alliance between the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-NYPD officer sentenced to 18 months after conviction for helping China stalk an expat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former New York police sergeant was sentenced Wednesday to 18 months in prison in a U.S. case about China's pursuit of critics abroad, a sentence that came after two members of Congress urged the judge to spare him from time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Island-wide blackout hits Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — An island-wide blackout hit Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the largely Catholic residents of the U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MrBeast fans seek refunds after Las Vegas event
item.description: A crowd of frustrated "MrBeast" fans gathered Tuesday in Las Vegas to demand refunds for a three-day experience.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, WGN 9
